findings.title: Hot access paths now construct a much heavier recorder state even when sampling is off
findings.impact: The default all-1.0 configuration disables sampling, but every converted client/worker access path still constructs ObjectAccessRecorder. That now includes ObjectAccessState with many std::string and std::function members before the actual operation starts, adding per-request constructor/destructor/cache footprint on hot paths such as KVClient::Create/Set/Get where the old AccessRecorder only held timing/name/type state and built RequestParam at record time.
findings.fix: Keep the facade state out of the unconditional hot-path object, for example by making ObjectAccessRecorder hold an optional/unique state allocated or constructed only after AccessRecorder::ShouldRecordInternal() is true, or by splitting a small fast guard from the heavier lazy field container.
